column4:
Character styles: all
Background: colour, opacity, SVG added within the background container (e.g. logo watermark)
column5:
☐ On mobile (up to 1259px), the HTML content is always positioned below the image/video content (e.g. IMG+HTML and HTML+IMG look the same on mobile).
☐ The image carousel content template should always feature a gradient above the image and beneath the pagination.
☐ Add a new field to CMS that allows you to add either a cover image or a video preview (optional, if not added, the video player fills the space) 
☐ When the video is clicked, it opens a pop-up.
☐ Template has a minimum height regardless of how little HTML has been added:
   1. Range 1 (media above HTML):
   - screen 375px-1259px: n/a, follow Padding Rule 2 (below)
   2. Range 2 (media next to HTML): 
   - screen 1260px: minimum 560px height
   - screen 2560px: minimum 840px height

☐ PADDING RULE (internal)
Approx Internal Padding (above and below the HTML) should be:
• Range 1 (media above HTML):
screen 375px: 40px padding
screen 1259px: 85px padding
• Range 2 (media next to HTML): 
screen 1260px: 85px padding (above & below HTML)
screen 2560px: 200px padding (above & below HTML)

☐ MARGIN RULE (external)
Reduce margin to 0px when the content template is placed above or below an element that is full-width and has a background. These include:
- another half-half content template*,
- full-width content template,
- full-width video,
- header (all types),
- featured Paragraph (variant 1),
- event full-width promo (both variants),
- admissions calculator (variant 1),
- tickertape (variant 2),
- notices list (both variants),
- events list (both variants)
- donations calculator,
- quote carousel (variant 1),
- widget carousel with a background (Upcoming Events, Related Stories).

* If the designer chooses variant 2 for the half-half content templates (which don't have a background), reduce the padding to 50% instead of changing it to 0px

☐ All portals in the carousel should be top-aligned. If a promo contains more text than others, the additional text should wrap and extend downward without affecting the top alignment.
☐ Unlimited number of portals can be added
☐ Carousel isn't looping
☐ The appropriate arrow is "greyed out" and no longer clickable when you get to either end of the carousel
☐ The carousel arrows don't appear when all elements are fully visible

☐ The grid adapts to different numbers of portals as per the design:
    - The maximum number of portals in a row is 3
    - When just 1 portal has been added, it stretches to match the width of 3 promos, and the title appears within the rectangle rather than underneath it
    - When the number of portals is not divisible by 3, the odd 1 or 2 portals stretch to match the width of a 3-portal row
    - The extended portals always appear in the first row
☐ On mobile, an odd number of portals ends with a wider promo at the end of the grid
☐ All promos in a row should be top-aligned. If a promo contains more text than others, the additional text should wrap and extend downward without affecting the top alignment; it will simply push down the following row.
